description:
You are acting as a backup-integrity engineer.  
    Inside the directory /home/user/backups there is an existing CSV file called
    backup_report.csv whose content is **exactly** (including the newline at the
    end of the last line):

        BackupID,Date,Status,SizeMB
        BKP001,2023-07-01,OK,512
        BKP002,2023-07-02,OK,514
        BKP003,2023-07-03,FAILED,0
        BKP004,2023-07-04,OK,518
        BKP005,2023-07-05,OK,520

    Your tasks:

    1. Use a single terminal command that combines `cut` (and, if you wish,
       redirection operators) to extract only the first and third columns
       (BackupID and Status) from backup_report.csv, **keeping the original
       header**.  
       • Fields are comma-separated.  
       • Do **not** reorder the columns; keep BackupID first, Status second.  

    2. Save the extracted two-column data into a new file named
       /home/user/backups/backup_id_status.txt.  
       The new file must end up with **exactly** six lines and must look
       precisely like this (including the trailing newline on the last line):

        BackupID,Status
        BKP001,OK
        BKP002,OK
        BKP003,FAILED
        BKP004,OK
        BKP005,OK

    3. Create a one-line log file called /home/user/backups/backup_extract.log
       that reports how many data lines (lines excluding the header) were placed
       in backup_id_status.txt.  
       The log file must contain **exactly** the single line shown below,
       followed by a single newline character and nothing else:

        records_extracted=5

       NO spaces, tabs, timestamps, or additional text are allowed.  
       Remember: the test harness will check the file content **byte for byte**.

    Hints & constraints:

    • Accomplish step 1 with a *single* pipeline/command (excluding the log file
      creation).  
    • Do not edit backup_report.csv; only read from it.  
    • Do not hard-code the five data-line count into backup_id_status.txt; rely
      on the cut operation to copy whatever is present.  
    • You do not need root privileges.  
    • After you finish, the directory /home/user/backups should contain all three
      files: the original backup_report.csv and your two new files,
      backup_id_status.txt and backup_extract.log, with the exact contents
      outlined above.

    When everything is correct, the automated checker will:

    • Confirm that /home/user/backups/backup_id_status.txt exists and matches
      the exact six-line text block given above.  
    • Confirm that /home/user/backups/backup_extract.log exists and contains the
      exact single line “records_extracted=5\n”.

    Proceed in the terminal to complete the task.
truth:
<!-- Existing file before the agent starts -->
    <file path="/home/user/backups/backup_report.csv"><![CDATA[
BackupID,Date,Status,SizeMB
BKP001,2023-07-01,OK,512
BKP002,2023-07-02,OK,514
BKP003,2023-07-03,FAILED,0
BKP004,2023-07-04,OK,518
BKP005,2023-07-05,OK,520
]]></file>

    <!-- Files that must exist after agent completes the task -->
    <file path="/home/user/backups/backup_id_status.txt"><![CDATA[
BackupID,Status
BKP001,OK
BKP002,OK
BKP003,FAILED
BKP004,OK
BKP005,OK
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/backups/backup_extract.log"><![CDATA[
records_extracted=5
]]></file>
